applicant_and_family_particulars.has_applicant_ever_changed_name_details: No legal name change has ever been made. The applicant was born as “Jonathan Michael Carter” on his original Colorado birth certificate and continues to use this full legal name on all official documents, including his current U.S. passport, Social Security records, and driver’s license. The only variations that exist are informal or shortened versions such as “Jon” or “Jon Carter,” which appear in casual contexts (e.g., email display name, social media, and how friends address him). However, he has never filed any petition for a legal name change in any court, nor has he changed his name due to marriage, divorce, adoption, or any other legal process. All academic records (high school transcripts from East High School in Denver, CO, and university transcripts from the University of Washington in Seattle, WA) are also issued under the same full legal name, “Jonathan Michael Carter.”
applicant_and_family_particulars.citizenship_of_specified_countries_detai:
N/A – Neither the applicant nor his parents or grandparents have ever held citizenship of Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Bhutan, China, Nepal, or Sri Lanka. The applicant’s family background is as follows:

• Applicant: U.S. citizen by birth, born in Denver, Colorado, USA.
• Father: David Alan Carter, born in Omaha, Nebraska, USA – U.S. citizen by birth, never held any other citizenship.
• Mother: Linda Marie Carter (née Reynolds), born in Des Moines, Iowa, USA – U.S. citizen by birth, never held any other citizenship.
• Paternal grandparents: Robert James Carter (born in Iowa, USA) and Margaret Elaine Carter (born in Kansas, USA), both lifelong U.S. citizens.
• Maternal grandparents: Thomas Edward Reynolds (born in Illinois, USA) and Patricia Ann Reynolds (born in Missouri, USA), both lifelong U.S. citizens.

No member of the immediate or extended family has resided in or applied for citizenship in Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Bhutan, China, Nepal, or Sri Lanka, nor do they possess passports or national identity documents from any of these countries.
applicant_and_family_particulars.citizenship_of_pakistan_nicop_poc_detail:
N/A – The applicant, his parents, and his grandparents have never held Pakistani citizenship, NICOP (National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis), POC (Pakistan Origin Card), or any form of Pakistani national identity or travel document.

The family has not lived in Pakistan, has no ancestral roots there to their knowledge, and has never registered with any Pakistani authority. All known family records (birth certificates, passports, and national identification documents) are from the United States only. The applicant has visited Pakistan once for business in 2022 on a U.S. passport with a regular tourist/business visa, but this was strictly a short-term visit and did not involve any application for citizenship, NICOP, POC, or similar status.

employment_and_official_status.current_employment_status_and_employer_d:
Current Employment Status: Employed full-time.

Job Title: Senior Software Engineer (Backend & Cloud Infrastructure)

Employer Name: Cascade Analytics, Inc.

Employer Address:
Cascade Analytics, Inc.
1207 4th Avenue, Suite 900
Seattle, WA 98101
United States of America

Work Contact Details:
• Office Phone: (206) 555-7842
• Direct Line: (206) 555-3198
• HR Department Phone: (206) 555-7700
• Work Email: jonathan.carter@cascadeanalytics.com
• Company Website: https://www.cascadeanalytics.com

Employment Details (for context and clarity):
The applicant has been employed by Cascade Analytics, Inc., a mid-sized data analytics and cloud services company based in Seattle, Washington, since 2020-07-15. His role primarily involves designing and maintaining backend microservices, integrating with cloud infrastructure (primarily AWS), and ensuring data security and compliance for enterprise clients in healthcare and retail.

He works on a hybrid schedule, typically on-site at the Seattle office three days per week and remotely from his home in Bellevue, Washington, two days per week. His responsibilities include:
• Architecting and implementing RESTful APIs in Python and Go.
• Managing AWS infrastructure (EC2, RDS, S3, Lambda, IAM) for several client-facing products.
• Leading a small team of three junior engineers, conducting code reviews, and mentoring them.
• Coordinating with the compliance team to align data handling practices with HIPAA and GDPR guidelines.
• Participating in bi-weekly client calls to provide technical updates and gather requirements.

Previous Employment (brief overview for completeness):
Before joining Cascade Analytics, Inc., the applicant worked as a Software Engineer at Pinecrest Digital Solutions, LLC in Denver, Colorado, from 2016-08-01 to 2020-06-30. That employment ended amicably when he relocated to the Seattle area for his spouse’s job and for better career opportunities in the tech sector.

Current Residential Address (for reference, although not explicitly requested in this section):

legal_and_asylum_history.criminal_offense_or_charges_details:
No – The applicant has never been convicted of any criminal offense, and there are no ongoing criminal charges or investigations against him in the United States or any other country.

Details:
• He has a clean criminal record with no arrests for felonies or misdemeanors.
• Driving record: He received a single minor speeding ticket in Colorado in 2014 for driving 8 mph over the speed limit; this was resolved by paying the fine, and no criminal proceedings were involved.
• He has never been involved in any court case as a defendant in criminal matters, nor has he been placed on probation or parole.
• He has never been denied a visa nor deported or removed from any country, and he has never overstayed a visa.

For employment background checks, including those conducted by Cascade Analytics, Inc. in 2020 and Pinecrest Digital Solutions, LLC in 2016, his record has consistently come back clear with no criminal history reported.
legal_and_asylum_history.asylum_application_details:
No – Neither the applicant nor his parents have ever applied for asylum in any country.

Details:
• The applicant has always resided lawfully in the United States, apart from temporary stays abroad for tourism or short business trips.
• He has never sought refugee status, political asylum, or any similar protection in the United States, India, or any third country.
• His parents, David Alan Carter and Linda Marie Carter, have also never applied for asylum, refugee status, or any humanitarian protection program in any country.
• There are no known family members (siblings or grandparents) who have pursued asylum claims.

All international travel by the applicant has been conducted using his standard U.S. passport with the appropriate visas (tourist or business) obtained in advance when required, and all stays have been within the permitted duration indicated by the immigration authorities of the respective countries visited.
